title: Bound states of solitons in fiber lasers
authors: Yudong Cui, Tianchang Lu, Yusheng Zhang, Dong Mao, Boris A. Malomed
abstract: This article presents a systematic review of theoretical and experimental findings for bound states of two and several dissipative solitons in fiber lasers. The theoretical basis underlying the formation and stabilization of soliton molecules in the fibers, which is provided by the complex Ginzburg-Landau equations and bound states of such equations, is presented in necessary detail, which is followed by a detailed presentation of experimental findings, including very recent ones. In particular, included are the results for the multi-soliton bound states in the fibers, as well as for the bound states in the temporal and frequency domains, single-component (scalar) and two-component (vector), two- and multi-soliton modes, as well as for bound states of spatiotemporal dissipative solitons in the lasers based on multimode fibers.
summary: This review provides a comprehensive overview of how dissipative solitons form bound states in fiber lasers. It covers the mathematical foundation using Ginzburg-Landau equations and summarizes recent experimental progress in multi-soliton and vector soliton dynamics.
main_problem: The paper reviews the formation, stabilization, and various configurations of bound states of dissipative solitons (soliton molecules) in fiber lasers.
main_result: A systematic review of theoretical frameworks and experimental evidence for multi-soliton bound states across temporal, frequency, scalar, and vector domains.
method: The review synthesizes theoretical analysis using the complex Ginzburg-Landau equations with recent experimental findings in fiber laser systems.
model_or_system: The physical systems studied include dissipative solitons in fiber lasers, specifically focusing on single-component (scalar) and two-component (vector) modes, as well as spatiotemporal solitons in multimode fibers.
key_observables: Temporal and frequency domain characteristics of soliton bound states, and multi-soliton mode configurations.
paper_structure: The paper begins with the theoretical basis of soliton molecules via complex Ginzburg-Landau equations, followed by a detailed presentation of experimental results including temporal/frequency domains, scalar/vector components, and multimode fiber dynamics.
